linkedin_profile.headline: Global Commercial Director at The Twenty Two
linkedin_profile.about: A proven sales professional, focusing on strategic multi-market management within the luxury hotel industry. Commercially driven with over a decade of experience in on-property and global sales positions, covering the Entertainment, Leisure, MICE, & Business Travel segments. An outgoing, positive and socially confident person who receives energy from being around others. This translates into my passion for sales - connecting and networking with people in an international marketplace. Having grown up in a very welcoming and hospitable family, my passion for hospitality started at a young age. Graduating from Bournemouth University with a Bachelors of Science Degree in Hospitality Management in 2013, I have enjoyed mastering my expertise within the sales function of the 5 Star Hotel market since then. I am passionate about Music, Food and Sport. From the age of 12 to 16 I represented the United Kingdom Hockey team, gaining 8 caps for my country, whilst also captaining the South East of England Regional team and Buckinghamshire County teams from 10 to 17.

experience.title: Director Of Front Office
experience.description: Spearheading the launch of The Twenty Two’s second property in New York following the successful opening of its flagship in London. As Director of Front Office, I oversee all front-of-house operations, ensuring a seamless and exceptional guest experience that embodies the brand’s commitment to personalised luxury. Key responsibilities include: - Leading and developing a world-class Front Office team, including Guest Relations, Experience Coordinators, and Reservations. - Driving service excellence, operational efficiency, and guest satisfaction in a dynamic pre-opening environment. - Collaborating cross-functionally with Housekeeping, Engineering, and Food & Beverage to maintain the highest standards. - Implementing strategic initiatives to enhance the guest journey and elevate brand positioning in the New York market. Notable achievements: - Integral in the pre-opening and successful launch of The Twenty Two London, establishing it as a premier luxury destination. - Key player in expanding The Twenty Two brand to New York, overseeing recruitment, training, and operational readiness for the opening phase. - Developing service strategies tailored to the demands of a high-profile clientele, ensuring an unparalleled guest experience. Passionate about luxury hospitality, team development, and curating memorable stays that define The Twenty Two’s unique identity.

linkedin_profile.headline: Director Of Operations at The Twenty Two
linkedin_profile.about:
I've studied Catering, Hotel and Restaurant Management in my native France whilst beginning my waiting career in hotels and restaurants across central France. Following a move to the UK in 2007, i've joined Pearl, the then Chancery Court hotel and fine dining restaurant. From there, I've joined Lutyens on Fleet Street (the Prescott & Conran venue) as Assistant Manager, overseeing a large team across two restaurants and two bars. Gaining valuable experience as acting General Manager for a portion of my time with the company, I was later promoted to Private Club & Private Dining Manager. Where I exclusively managed the four private dining rooms and all-day members club. In 2015, I have joined as General Manager at The Ninth restaurant by the celebrity chef Jun Tanaka. The Ninth is an award winning 1 Michelin start restaurant and 3 AA rosettes. Located on Charlotte street in the heart of London’s buzzy Fitzrovia neighbourhood, the contemporary, yet warm and inviting 90-seater restaurant offers up simple but refined French Mediterranean-style dishes, all made for sharing. From 2018, I've joined the Frenchie Group team in London as a General Manager of its restaurant in Covent Garden. SInce December 2019, I am been promoted to Operations Director to FHG and taking care of all operations between France, UK and all other projects within the group.

Total Experience: 13 years and 2 months

experience.description: As Director of Front Office for The Twenty Two, I lead all guest-facing operational departments including Front Desk, Guest Relations, Concierge, Experience Coordinators (Butlers), Bellhops, and Room Reservations. One of only two team members relocated to New York to support the U.S. opening, I was originally part of the London preopening team that helped define the brand’s ethos and service standards from the ground up. I work closely with leadership to ensure all departments operate cohesively, with a strong emphasis on emotional intelligence, professionalism, and consistency. My goal is to create an environment where both guests and staff feel seen, supported, and valued.

linkedin_profile.about: With over a decade of experience in the food and beverage industry, I have had the privilege of working at some of the most prestigious establishments, including The Ritz Hotel, Annabel's Mayfair, and The Goring Hotel. My career has been shaped by my dedication to providing exceptional service while leading and inspiring teams to deliver the highest standards of excellence. experience.title: Director Of Engineering

Department: Engineering and Technical • Level: Director

experience.title: Regional Director Engineering And Maintenance

Ensured the timely completion of maintenance and repairs as well as compliance to regulations

experience.title: Director Of Engineering Maintenance At The Curtain Hotel Membersclub
experience.description: • Successfully managed the £75 Million construction and opening of The Curtain hotel and members club with 120 bedrooms, two floors of membership space, events space, private screening room and rooftop bar and pool. • Assigned capital expenditure project responsibility circa £500K for the support, redesign and completion from conception to implementation. • Managed snagging, certified all performance tests and conducted operating trials during the commission phase. • Negotiated with design build contractors and ensured that the project was delivered within schedule and budget. • Delivered training on safety, repair, GDPR compliance and maintenance, assessed performance and recognised individuals’ needs for further coaching and training. • Promoted involvement in all HSE activities, initiatives and programmes including ensuring the completion of fire inspections. Maintained records and licenses for the hotel as the Designated License Holder (DPS).

experience.title: Front Office Manager
experience.organization: The Standard Hotel, Highline - Andre Balazs Properties
experience.location: Greater New York City Area; Miami Florida, Shelter Island New York
experience.description: Recruited from previous internship with Andre Balazs Properties. Developed front of house team focusing on recruitment, training, and development for 337-room boutique hotel. Additionally, was asked to task force two years in a row, for seasonal opening of the Sunset Beach Hotel in Shelter Island as well as front office management task force at Standard, Miami Beach. Promoted from Assistant Front Desk Manager to Front Desk Manager within the first 6 months after opening after showing strong control of front office department needs and flexibility to the challenges of the operation while focusing on the guest and employee experience.

experience.title: Lead Coordinator - Centre Of Business Engagement
experience.description: The position entailed working with various Managers and Coordinators within the department to manage and coordinate Industry events and initiatives. Additionally, helped on the Media Team to coordinate live Television Debates during the WEF in Davos, Press Junkets and Photographer communication. I was responsible for about 18 Team Coordinators to support and ensure the accurate progress of the projects and events.

experience.title: Director Of Rooms - Sydell Group
experience.description: Joined the pre-opening team for the 168-room luxury boutique hotel as Front Office Manager recruiting and training all employees for concierge, pbx, room service and bell team. Promoted to Director of Rooms in June, 2013 and continued to oversee 80 employees across front office, guest services, housekeeping and engineering departments. Additional assignment: opening task force in various operational roles at Line Los Angeles (398 rooms).

experience.title: General Manager At Casa Faena
experience.description: A short-term assignment responsible for all construction renovation items which included a total structural and decorative renovation of the historic 50-room property. Once reopened, managed the re-branding and marketing to a Faena branded property. Hotel was repositioned and staff retrained resulting in higher guest and staff satisfaction. One example of the successful retraining was our Tripadvisor rating from #202 to #9 in all of Miami Beach.

experience.title: Director Of Operations
experience.description: Due to my relationship with the Ian Schrager team from past projects, I took on a short-term, 1–year assignment to assist in opening the 273-room Edition Clocktower hotel. Reporting directly into the GM, I was responsible for all union operating departments including 40 housekeeping attendants, 8 front office agents, 3 concierges, 10 bellmen, 8 pbx/guest service agents, 6 room service servers and a team of about 30 in the lobby bar including managers, bartenders, bar backs and servers.

experience.title: Director Of Rooms - The Ned
experience.description: Collaborated with hotel General Manager in opening of 300K square foot luxury hotel with 272-rooms, nine restaurants, a fitness/wellness center, and private members club in the city of London. Created pre-opening budgets for guest rooms & hotel-related operations. Created standard operating procedures (SOPs), recruited, trained and managed the rooms team, including 40 housekeeping attendants, 30 front of house team members and 8 guest service agents out of a total of 700+ employees.

experience.title: General Manager - Sydell Group
experience.description: Reporting into the COO and CEO directly, I managed one of New York City’s most successful boutique hotels with 168-luxury rooms, 140-seat Michelin-starred restaurant and the James Beard award winning NoMad bar. Oversaw all aspects of hotel, including finance, sales, human resources, engineering, revenue, guest experience, events, capital expenditure, and budgets. Collaborated with restaurant General Manager to direct food and beverage (F&B). Pre-pandemic, along with my team of department heads, I was responsible for 320 employees in total. Promoted from Hotel Manager to General Manager in 2018. Additional assignments included opening task force for sister properties: Ned London (272 rooms), Line Washington DC (220 rooms), Nomad Las Vegas (293 rooms), Freehand New York (400 rooms) and the upcoming Nomad London (93 rooms). In 2020, closed and reopened Nomad, NY and Line DC due to pandemic and was involved in all strategic planning to get the properties safely and successfully through the pandemic. Some of these strategies in 2020 and into 2021 involved creating new “business models” for the property such as pivoting all F&B outdoors and developing a robust delivery service. Managed re-closure of Nomad New York property due to corporate restructuring in April, 2021.

experience.title: Founder
experience.description: Developed non profit website / organization giving cancer patients and caregivers access to discounted hotel rooms at various partner hotels across New York City, Boston and Miami. These rooms benefit patients by providing safe, comfortable and affordable environments for individuals undergoing treatment as well as create a sense of escapism that can assist the body and mind in the healing process.
